<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 512 512" width="512" height="512" fill="none">
  <!-- Background -->
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="#f0e6d2" />
  <path d="M0 350 Q256 300 512 350 V512 H0 Z" fill="#d2b48c" />

  <!-- Melting Clock 1 -->
  <path d="M150 200 C150 150, 250 150, 250 250 C250 350, 180 400, 150 380 C120 360, 150 250, 150 200" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="4" />
  <path d="M170 230 L200 260 M200 260 L220 230" stroke="black" stroke-width="3" stroke-linecap="round" />
  
  <!-- Melting Clock 2 (draped over edge) -->
  <path d="M350 320 C420 320, 450 350, 450 420 C450 480, 380 500, 350 480 C320 460, 320 320, 350 320" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="4" />
  <path d="M360 380 L390 410 M390 410 L410 390" stroke="black" stroke-width="3" stroke-linecap="round" />

  <!-- Floating Eye -->
  <ellipse cx="380" cy="150" rx="60" ry="35" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="3" />
  <circle cx="380" cy="150" r="20" fill="#4682b4" />
  <circle cx="380" cy="150" r="8" fill="black" />

  <!-- Floating Apple -->
  <path d="M100 100 C80 100, 70 130, 100 150 C130 130, 120 100, 100 100" fill="#6b8e23" stroke="#3e4d16" stroke-width="2" />
  <path d="M100 105 L105 90" stroke="#4b3621" stroke-width="3" />

  <!-- Distant thin tree/crutch -->
  <line x1="50" y1="340" x2="50" y2="150" stroke="black" stroke-width="2" />
  <path d="M30 150 Q50 130 70 150" stroke="black" stroke-width="2" fill="none" />
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}
